skill.name: postgresql-optimization
skill.description: PostgreSQL database optimization workflow for query tuning, indexing strategies, performance analysis, and production database management.
skill.summary: Complete workflow for PostgreSQL performance optimization including query analysis, indexing strategies, configuration tuning, and maintenance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 36 potential issues (external_commands, weak cryptographic algorithm) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill file contains only markdown documentation with skill references and prompts. No actual code execution, cryptographic operations, or security risks exist. The detected patterns are markdown code blocks and skill name references misinterpreted by the scanner.

content.value_statement: PostgreSQL databases can suffer from slow queries and poor performance without proper optimization. This skill provides a systematic workflow for query tuning, indexing, and configuration management.
actual_capabilities: Analyze PostgreSQL query performance using EXPLAIN ANALYZE
Design and implement indexing strategies including B-tree, composite, and partial indexes
Tune PostgreSQL configuration parameters like shared_buffers and work_mem
Schedule and manage VACUUM and ANALYZE maintenance operations
Set up monitoring and alerting for database performance metrics
Identify and resolve database bottlenecks through systematic assessment
limitations: Requires existing database access and appropriate permissions
Cannot modify application code that generates queries
Does not include hardware provisioning or infrastructure changes
Skill relies on other referenced skills for full functionality

use_cases.title: Resolve Slow Query Performance
use_cases.description: Systematically analyze and optimize slow-running queries in production PostgreSQL databases using EXPLAIN ANALYZE and indexing strategies.

use_cases.title: Design Indexing Strategy
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive indexing plans including B-tree, composite, and partial indexes to improve query performance across multiple tables.

use_cases.title: Tune PostgreSQL Configuration
use_cases.description: Optimize PostgreSQL server settings like shared_buffers, work_mem, and effective_cache_size for better resource utilization.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Performance Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @database-optimizer to assess PostgreSQL performance on my database. Check version, review configuration, and identify slow queries.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new PostgreSQL optimization project

prompt_templates.title: Query Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt:
Use @sql-optimization-patterns to analyze and optimize the following queries. Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE and suggest improvements:

```sql
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at > '2024-01-01';
```

prompt_templates.title: Indexing Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @database-design to design PostgreSQL indexing strategy for our e-commerce database with tables for users, orders, products, and reviews.
prompt_templates.scenario: Designing indexes for a new application

prompt_templates.title: Configuration Tuning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @postgres-best-practices to tune PostgreSQL configuration for a database server with 8GB RAM serving 500 concurrent users.

output_examples.input: Optimize PostgreSQL queries on our orders table
output: - Identified 3 slow queries with execution time > 500ms
- Missing indexes detected on orders.customer_id and orders.status
2. Create partial index for active orders
3. Add composite index for frequent query pattern
- Increase work_mem to 64MB for sort operations
- Set effective_cache_size to 6GB (75% of available memory)

output_examples.input: Design indexing strategy for analytics queries
output: - B-tree on events(user_id, created_at) for user activity
- B-tree on events(event_type, created_at) for event filtering
- Index on orders WHERE status = 'pending' for processing queue

best_practices: Always run EXPLAIN ANALYZE before and after query optimizations to measure actual improvements
Create indexes during low-traffic periods as index creation locks tables
Monitor query performance over time using pg_stat_statements to identify regression
anti_patterns: Creating too many indexes - each index adds write overhead and consumes storage
Using sequential scans when indexes are available due to misconfiguration
Ignoring table bloat and vacuum settings - leads to performance degradation over time

faq.question: What PostgreSQL versions are supported?
faq.answer: This workflow supports PostgreSQL 9.6 and later versions, including PostgreSQL 14, 15, and 16.

faq.question: Does this skill require database admin privileges?
faq.answer: Yes, some operations require superuser or owner privileges for configuration changes and maintenance tasks.

faq.question: Can this skill optimize queries in any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill optimizes the SQL queries themselves regardless of the application language (Python, Node.js, Ruby, etc.).

faq.question: How long does a typical optimization project take?
faq.answer: A basic assessment takes 1-2 hours. Full optimization including indexing and tuning may take 1-3 days depending on database size.

faq.question: Will creating indexes cause downtime?
faq.answer: CREATE INDEX without CONCURRENTLY can lock writes. Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY for production databases to avoid downtime.

faq.question: How do I monitor improvements after optimization?
faq.answer: Use pg_stat_statements to track query times, set up pgBadger for log analysis, and create Grafana dashboards for ongoing monitoring.
